rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks into valid component or implementation scopes
Use implementation scope only for bindings to existing output features or declared auxiliary variables
Place component type contracts in component type annexes and implementation-level bindings in implementation annexes
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the scope of the intended target component or implementation
Use valid AADL feature names and AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) in contracts
Avoid using AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic in AGREE contracts
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks use the syntax `annex agree {** ... **};`
Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence, e.g., `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`
Use AGREE logical operators such as `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>`
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions
